post.description:
Register a new user.
Public endpoint - historization is skipped (no user context).
Mail / password login endpoint.
OAuth2 token endpoint for login (required by FastAPI).

get.description: Retrieve the current logged-in user information.
Protected route to test authentication.
List all users with pagination.

schema.title: Response List Users Auth Users Get

post.description:
Logout endpoint that revokes the current authentication token.

Supports both JWT tokens (from /auth/login) and OAuth tokens (from /oauth/token).
The token will be added to a blacklist and will no longer be valid for authentication.

OAuth2 token endpoint for client credentials flow.

This endpoint follows the OAuth2 client credentials grant specification:
https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6749#section-4.4

get.description: List all OAuth clients (admin only).

schema.title: Response List Oauth Clients Oauth Clients Get

post.description: Create a new OAuth client (admin only).

get.description: Get OAuth client details (admin only).

delete.description: Revoke an OAuth client and all its tokens (admin only).

post.description: Revoke a specific access token (admin only).
Clean up expired OAuth tokens (admin only).

get.description:
Retrieve all available roles in the system.
ADMIN role required.

schema.title: Response Get All Roles Admin Roles  Get

put.description:
Update a user's role.
ADMIN role required.
Uses session with user context for historization.

get.description:
Retrieve the role for a user.
ADMIN role required.

schema.title: Response Get User Role Admin Roles User  User Id  Get

get.description:
Retrieve the current user's profile.
Accessible to all authenticated users.

Retrieve a list of all users with cursor-based pagination.
ADMIN role required.

Retrieve a user by their ID.
Users can only see their own profile,
admins can see all profiles.

post.description: Create a new community (admin only).

get.description:
Get communities with cursor-based pagination.
Collectivite users only see their accessible communities.
Get a specific community by unique_id (only if user has access).
Get a specific community by ID (only if user has access).

post.description: Assign a user to a community (admin only).

delete.description: Remove a user from a community (admin only).

post.description: Add a parent community to a community.

delete.description: Remove a parent community from a community.

get.description: Get all parent communities.

schema.title: Response Get Community Parents Communities  Community Id  Parents Get

get.description: Get all child communities.

schema.title: Response Get Community Children Communities  Community Id  Children Get

post.description: Create a new funding program (ADMIN only).

get.description:
Get all funding programs with cursor-based pagination.
Accessible to all authenticated users.
Get a specific funding program by ID (accessible to all authenticated users).

post.description:
Create a new action model (ADMIN only).
Can optionally associate with funding programs by providing their IDs.

get.description:
Get all action models with cursor-based pagination and optional filtering.
Accessible to all authenticated users.
Get a specific action model by ID (accessible to all authenticated users).

put.description:
Update an action model (ADMIN only).
Can update funding program associations by providing new funding program IDs.

delete.description:
Delete an action model (ADMIN only).
This will also remove all funding program associations.

get.summary: Get Action Models By Funding Program
get.description:
Get all action models associated with a specific funding program with cursor-based pagination.
Accessible to all authenticated users.

post.description:
Create a new action assigned to the user's main community.
Must reference an existing action model.

get.description:
Get actions accessible to the user with cursor-based pagination.
Actions are filtered by user's accessible communities.
Get a specific action by unique_id (only if user has access to its community).
Get all indicators for a specific action.

schema.title: Response Get Action Indicators Actions  Action Id  Indicators Get

get.description: Get full action context for evaluating JSONLogic indicator rules.
Get a specific action by ID (only if user has access to its community).

put.description: Update an action (only if user has access to its community).

delete.description: Delete an action (only if user has access to its community).

get.description:
Get all actions associated with a specific action model with cursor-based pagination.
Actions are filtered by user's accessible communities.

put.description:
Update the complete list of beneficiaries for an action.
Replaces all existing beneficiaries with the provided list.

delete.description: Remove a specific beneficiary from an action.

post.description:
Create a new action theme (admin only).
The theme is created with DRAFT status and a generated unique_id.

get.description:
Get all action themes with cursor-based pagination and optional filtering.
By default, excludes deleted action themes unless explicitly filtered.
Accessible to all authenticated users.

get.summary: Get Action Theme By Unique Id
get.description: Get a specific action theme by unique_id (accessible to all authenticated users).
Get a specific action theme by ID (accessible to all authenticated users).

delete.description:
Soft delete an action theme (admin only).
Raises 409 Conflict if the theme has linked action models.

get.description:
Get all action models linked to a specific action theme with cursor-based pagination.
Accessible to all authenticated users.

post.description:
Duplicate an action theme (admin only).
The copy is created with DRAFT status and a new unique_id.

put.description:
Publish an action theme (admin only).
Changes status from DRAFT to PUBLISHED.

Disable an action theme (admin only).
Changes status from PUBLISHED to DISABLED.

Activate (re-publish) an action theme (admin only).
Changes status from DISABLED to PUBLISHED.

post.description:
Create a new folder model (ADMIN only).
Can optionally associate with funding programs by providing their IDs.

get.description:
Get all folder models with cursor-based pagination and optional filtering.
Accessible to all authenticated users.
Get a specific folder model by ID (accessible to all authenticated users).

put.description:
Update a folder model (ADMIN only).
Can update funding program associations by providing new funding program IDs.

delete.description:
Delete a folder model (ADMIN only).
This will also remove all funding program associations.

get.summary: Get Folder Models By Funding Program
get.description:
Get all folder models associated with a specific funding program with cursor-based pagination.
Accessible to all authenticated users.

post.description: Create a new folder assigned to the user's main community.

get.description:
Get folders accessible to the user with cursor-based pagination.
Folders are filtered by user's accessible communities.

Get a specific folder by unique_id (only if user has access to its community).
Returns folder with next_possible_statuses field indicating allowed transitions.

Get a specific folder by ID (only if user has access to its community).
Returns folder with next_possible_statuses field indicating allowed transitions.

put.description: Update a folder (only if user has access to its community).

delete.description: Delete a folder (only if user has access to its community).

get.description:
Get folders by status with cursor-based pagination.
Folders are filtered by user's accessible communities.

Get folders by funding program with cursor-based pagination.
Folders are filtered by user's accessible communities.

post.description:
Create a new agent.

If community_id is not provided, the agent will be assigned to the user's main community.

get.description:
Get agents accessible to the user with cursor-based pagination.

Agents are filtered by user's accessible communities.
By default, DELETED agents are excluded.
Get an agent by ID.

put.description:
Update an agent.

Status transitions:
- Only DELETED can be set manually
- DRAFT and COMPLETED are determined automatically by field values

delete.description:
Soft delete an agent (set status to DELETED).

This is a soft delete - the agent remains in the database but with DELETED status.

get.description: Get an agent by unique_id (XXX11 format).

post.description:
Create a new indicator model (ADMIN only).
Can optionally associate with action models by providing their IDs.

If type is TEXT, the unit field is automatically ignored.

get.description:
Get all indicator models with cursor-based pagination and optional filtering.
Accessible to all authenticated users.

get.summary: Get Indicator Models By Action Model
get.description:
Get all indicator models associated with a specific action model with cursor-based pagination.
Accessible to all authenticated users.
Get a specific indicator model by ID (accessible to all authenticated users).

put.description:
Update an indicator model (ADMIN only).
Can update action model associations by providing new action model IDs.

If type changes to TEXT, the unit field is automatically set to None.

delete.description:
Delete an indicator model (ADMIN only).
This will also remove all action model associations.

get.description:
Get a specific indicator by ID.
Returns indicator details including the associated indicator model information.

put.description:
Update an indicator's value.

For TEXT type indicators, use `value_text`.
For NUMBER type indicators, use `value_number`.

Validation ensures that only the appropriate value type is set based on the indicator model type.

delete.description:
Delete an indicator.
Cannot delete a child indicator individually or the last indicator of a type.
If the indicator is a GROUP, its children are deleted via cascade.

post.description:
Duplicate an indicator. If the indicator is a GROUP, its children are also duplicated.
Requires the indicator to have duplicable_enabled in its action model link.

get.description:
Retrieve recent activities from the system with optional filters.

    **Use cases**:
    - Global activity feed
    - Activities by a specific user
    - Activities on a specific entity type
    - Activities filtered by action type (CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE)
    - Activities since a specific date

    **Pagination**: Uses cursor-based pagination with HATEOAS links.

parameters.name: entity_type

schema.description: Filter by entity type (Folder, Action, User, etc.)
schema.title: Entity Type

parameters.description: Filter by entity type (Folder, Action, User, etc.)

parameters.name: action

schema.description: Filter by action type (create, update, delete)
schema.title: Action

parameters.description: Filter by action type (create, update, delete)

parameters.name: since

schema.description: Filter activities after this date (ISO 8601 format)
schema.title: Since

parameters.description: Filter activities after this date (ISO 8601 format)

200.description: List of activities with pagination metadata

400.description: Invalid parameters (e.g., invalid entity_type or cursor)

get.summary: Activities since a specific date (for notifications)
get.description:
Retrieve activities since a given date, excluding the current user's own actions.

    **Use case**: "What happened since my last visit" notifications.
    Shows activities from other users while the current user was away.

    **Note**: The `since` parameter is required. Use ISO 8601 format
    (e.g., 2025-12-19T14:30:00Z).

parameters.name: since

schema.description: Filter activities after this date (ISO 8601 format, required)
schema.title: Since

parameters.description: Filter activities after this date (ISO 8601 format, required)

200.description: List of activities with pagination metadata

get.description:
Count activities matching the given filters without fetching all data.

    **Use case**: Check if there are new activities since last visit
    (e.g., badge showing "12 new updates").

parameters.name: exclude_current_user

schema.description: Exclude current user's activities from count
schema.title: Exclude Current User

parameters.description: Exclude current user's activities from count

get.description:
Retrieve the complete activity history for a specific entity.

    **Use case**: "History" tab on a folder, action, or any entity page,
    showing all changes made to that entity over time.

    **Entity types**: Folder, Action, User, Role, FundingProgram, ActionModel,
    FolderModel, Community, ActionTheme, Agent

parameters.name: entity_type

schema.description: Type of entity (Folder, Action, User, etc.)
schema.title: Entity Type

parameters.description: Type of entity (Folder, Action, User, etc.)

get.description:
Retrieve all activities related to a folder, including activities
    on child entities (e.g., actions within the folder).

    **Use case**: Complete project timeline showing all changes to the folder
    and its related actions.

    This includes:
    - Direct activities on the folder (creation, updates, status changes)
    - Activities on child entities (actions) that belong to this folder

Retrieve all available historical snapshots for an entity.

    **Use cases**:
    - Display a timeline of versions
    - Select a version to view
    - See when snapshots were created

    Snapshots are created automatically during critical changes
    (status closed, deletions, etc.) or manually.

    Returns versions in reverse chronological order (most recent first).

parameters.name: include_user_info

schema.description: Include user name who created each snapshot
schema.title: Include User Info

parameters.description: Include user name who created each snapshot

schema.title: Response Get Entity Versions History  Entity Type   Entity Id  Versions Get

get.description:
Retrieve the most recent historical snapshot for an entity.

    **Use case**: See the last saved state of the entity.

    Returns 404 if no snapshots exist for this entity.

404.description: No snapshots found for this entity

get.description:
Retrieve the state of an entity as it existed at a specific date.

    Finds the most recent snapshot created before or at the target date
    and returns the complete entity state.

    **Use case**: View a read-only form with historical values.

    **Date format**: ISO 8601 (e.g., 2025-12-01T10:30:00Z)

    Returns 404 if no snapshot exists for the entity before the given date.

200.description: Entity state at the specified date

404.description: No snapshot found for this entity at or before the date

get.description:
Compare two historical states of an entity to see what changed.

    Retrieves the entity state at both dates and produces a structured
    diff showing which fields changed, were added, or were removed.

    **Use case**: Diff interface with highlighted changes
    (green for new values, red for old values).

    **Date format**: ISO 8601 (e.g., 2025-12-01T10:30:00Z)

    Returns 404 if no snapshot is found for either date.

404.description: No snapshot found for one or both dates

get.description:
Count the number of available snapshots for an entity.

    **Use case**: Display version count in UI without fetching all versions.

post.description:
Seed the database with fake data for development.

This endpoint is only available in non-production environments.
It populates the database with a mix of fixed and random data:
- Communities (9 total: 4 fixed including default + 5 random French cities)
- Users (1 admin + 1 test user + 1 CDM user + 5 random users)
- Funding Programs (5-8)
- Action Themes (5-7)
- Action Models (8-10)
- Folder Models (5-7)
- Actions (10-15)
- Folders (10-15)
- Buildings (5-8)

The seeding is idempotent: it only inserts data if tables are empty.

Returns:
    dict: Summary of created entities with counts

Cursors are base64-encoded JSON objects containing created_at and id
